Video.summary: Gold built South Africa, but mining kills some who dig for it; in Stilfontein, a disaster that trapped miners underground reveals how a quest for gold became a story of hunger, survival and death.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged links to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Francine Lacqua visits Finland, where the threat from neighboring Russia looms large; she speaks to President Alexander Stubb about Finnish resilience and how his country is preparing for an uncertain future.
Video.grandparentTitle: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
Video.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland

Image.alt: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland

Video.summary: Francine Lacqua meets Ray Dalio on OceanX in the port of Nice for the UN Ocean Conference. They speak about radical transparency, building and leaving one of the largest hedge funds and what's next.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Francine Lacqua talks with Franklin Templeton CEO Jenny Johnson about how to steer a $1.6 trillion business amid the rise of AI, shifting politics and booming private markets.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Former U.K. Prime Minister Theresa May speaks with Francine Lacqua about Vladimir Putin and her approach to leadership in a world seeing a rise in strongmen.
Video.title: Episode 4

Video.summary: Scientists and entrepreneurs are testing radical ideas like solar radiation management and ocean fertilization; they are finding funding for them.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The idea of flying cars has largely remained science fiction, until now; a new generation of electrified aircraft is attracting the attention of investors and venture capitalists around the world.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A number of fatal car crashes involving Tesla vehicles has prompted increased scrutiny of its electric doors, triggering lawsuits over whether the design can leave passengers trapped.
Video.title: Tesla's Dangerous Doors

Video.summary: The International Finance Corp., an arm of the World Bank, spends millions of dollars of taxpayer money to build up private-sector services in the developing world.But a Bloomberg investigation reveals that several private hospital companies funded.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home
Video.title: The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home

Image.alt: The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home
The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home
The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home

Video.summary: Scientists are developing more powerful magnets to enable clean energy sources like fusion; China's dominance of the supply chain for rare earth magnets threatens their global availability.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Exploring the world of biocomputing; scientists are laying the foundation for a field that may blur the lines between the biological and synthetic.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Geothermal has been the underdog of the energy industry, but that may be about to change as new startups use fracking technologies to expand potential geothermal sites.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Governments and entrepreneurs are racing to commercialize the moon; these efforts could unlock a new, deep-space economy; but to return and stay will require the highest levels of innovation.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Emily Chang meets Snap CEO Evan Spiegel in Los Angeles for a conversation about his entrepreneurial journey, building Snap and his consumer hardware bet.
Video.title: Episode 14

Video.summary: Emily Chang goes inside Stargate, a $500 billion bet on the future of AI; she speaks with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and Softbank CEO Masayoshi Son about their hopes for the project.
Video.title: Episode 16

Video.summary: South Sudan relies on oil for almost all of its revenue. But some communities living near the oil fields allege polluted water is causing birth defects and miscarriages, raising questions over health and responsibility.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
Video.title: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom

Image.alt: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom

Video.summary: Emily Chang meets Disney Entertainment Co-Chair Dana Walden for an inside look at the entertainment giant's global streaming and TV ambitions.
Video.title: Episode 17

Video.summary: Emily Chang meets Reese Witherspoon and the Hello Sunshine executive team to discuss the company's founding mission and what's next for Hollywood.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: What drives the world's most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, we sit down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The sinking of the Gulf Livestock 1 was the worst disaster in the history of the live-export trade, an industry made up of about 150 ships with a market value totaling $20 billion to $30 billion.
Video.grandparentTitle: A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry
Video.title: A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry

Image.alt: A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry
A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry
A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry

Video.summary: Ji Chaoqun was a young engineer from Beijing heading to the US to study; then China’s spy agency came calling with bags of cash, the promise of glory and an offer that would change his life forever.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is supposed to root out graft; instead, some of its members have been accused of perpetuating the very wrongdoing they were meant to stop.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Emily Chang meets California's First Couple, Governor Gavin Newsom and Jennifer Siebel Newsom, visiting their home in Marin County and offices in Sacramento to see how they work together.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: How the son of a key adviser to Iran Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei built a secret oil empire.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: China's Controversial $167 Billion Mega-Dam
Video.title: China's Controversial $167 Billion Mega-Dam

Video.summary: Small towns are at the center of President Donald Trump's mass deportation campaign, spearheaded by ICE; in one rural New Mexico county, officials are grappling with what it means to be an ICE town.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Rise of America's ICE Towns
Video.title: The Rise of America's ICE Towns

Image.alt: The Rise of America's ICE Towns
The Rise of America's ICE Towns
The Rise of America's ICE Towns

Video.summary: Artificial intelligence has begun to exhibit early signs of agency.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bioacoustics technology and AI are revealing how animals communicate, and may soon allow humans to talk to them.
Video.title: Episode 2

Video.summary: With the promise of unimaginable computing power, a global race for quantum supremacy is raging.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Human survival depends on water, yet global reserves are rapidly shrinking; it's now replaced oil as the most likely cause of global conflict; discussing whether technology can help secure humanity's future water needs.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Pain is a fact of life; for some, it is excruciating and inescapable; but amid the continuing fallout of the opioid crisis, the development of new technologies aims to limit and even eliminate pain.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Soon, another billion people will be online, mostly from developing nations; ways new users will embrace this technology to transform their lives; its meaning for the rest of the world.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly talk with Emmanuel Acho about his reinvention from NFL linebacker to media leader, his show "Uncomfortable Conversations" and his new effort, Speakeasy.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly talk with USC Annenberg School for Communication and Journalism Dean Willow Bay about her investment in Angel City Football Club and her tenure on NBA Inside Stuff.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
Video.title: Episode 13

Video.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly sit with Super Bowl winner and Goldman Sachs Managing Director Justin Tuck to talk about the pressure of playing in New York and his transition from football to finance.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly sit with Erika Ayers Badan, former chief executive of Barstool Sports and current CEO of Food52, to discuss navigating turnarounds and how setbacks can fuel growth.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly interview Dallas Cowboys owner Jerry Jones on risk-taking, making bold trades and the Main Street philosophy that fueled his rise with the Cowboys.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
Video.title: Episode 11

Video.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly sit for a special conversation with two titans, football coach Bill Belichick and Bridgewater founder Ray Dalio, to learn their guiding principles for success.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A company majority-owned by billionaire Vincent Bolloré's Bolloré Group and Hubert Fabri is the subject of allegations of labor abuses at some of its African rubber plantations, including by female workers who say they were forced to engage in sex.
Video.grandparentTitle: Sex-for-Work Claims in West Africa's Rubber Industry
Video.title: Sex-for-Work Claims in West Africa's Rubber Industry

Image.alt: Sex-for-Work Claims in West Africa's Rubber Industry
Sex-for-Work Claims in West Africa's Rubber Industry
Sex-for-Work Claims in West Africa's Rubber Industry
